title: September Dark
content:
1
    The air falls chill;
    The whippoorwill
    Pipes lonesomely behind the Hill:
    The dusk grows dense,
    The silence tense;
    And lo, the katydids commence.

     2
    Through shadowy rifts
    Of woodland lifts
    The low, slow moon, and upward drifts,
    While left and right
    The fireflies' light
    Swirls eddying in the skirts of Night.

     3
    O Cloudland gray
    And level lay
    Thy mists across the face of Day!
    At foot and head,
    Above the dead
    O Dews, weep on uncomforted!
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, James Whitcomb Riley delivers a powerful performance in "September Dark"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
